serviceDescription.aws: Amazon Keyspaces (for Apache Cassandra) is a scalable, highly available, and managed Apache Cassandra–compatible database service. With Amazon Keyspaces, you can run your Cassandra workloads of any scale on AWS using the same Cassandra application code and developer tools that you use today.
serviceDescription.aws-us-gov: Amazon Keyspaces (for Apache Cassandra) is a scalable, highly available, and managed Apache Cassandra–compatible database service. With Amazon Keyspaces, you can run your Cassandra workloads of any scale on AWS using the same Cassandra application code and developer tools that you use today.
serviceDescription.aws-cn: Amazon Keyspaces (for Apache Cassandra) is a scalable, highly available, and managed Apache Cassandra–compatible database service. With Amazon Keyspaces, you can run your Cassandra workloads on Amazon Web Services by using the same Cassandra application code and developer tools that you use today. You don’t have to provision, patch, or manage servers, and you don’t have to install, maintain, or operate software.

templates.title: Keyspaces on-demand capacity
templates.description: With on-demand capacity mode, you do not need to specify how many reads and writes your application needs when you create your table. Amazon Keyspaces accommodates workloads instantly as they ramp up or down, making it a good option for applications with unpredictable traffic. With on-demand capacity you only pay for the reads and writes you use.

components.label: Storage
components.description: Enter the amount of storage for your keyspace in a given month. Note, unlike Cassandra. Amazon Keyspaces only charges for one copy of your data

components.label: Number of writes
components.description: Write operations include INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations. Each write request can write upto 1KB of data

operands.constantUnitLabel: write request units for each write

components.label: LOCAL_ONE reads
components.description: LOCAL_ONE consistency optimizes for performance and availability by returning the first returned value from any storage replica. Enter the percentage of your reads that will use LOCAL_ONE consistency

components.label: LOCAL_QUORUM reads
components.description: LOCAL_QUORUM consistency optimizes for data correctness by requiring at least two storage replicas to return a value before it is returned to your application. Enter the percentage of your reads that will use LOCAL_QUORUM consistency

components.header: Percentage total above is more than 100%
components.label: Percent alert

components.header: Percentage total above is less than 100%
components.label: Percent alert2
Number of reads
components.description: Each read request can read upto 4KB of data

operands.constantUnitLabel: read request units for LOCAL_ONE reads

components.outputUnitLabel: read request units for LOCAL_ONE reads

operands.constantUnitLabel: read request units for LOCAL_QUORUM reads

components.outputUnitLabel: read request units for LOCAL_QUORUM reads

operands.variableUnitLabel: read request units for LOCAL_ONE reads

operands.variableLabel: read request units for LOCAL_QUORUM reads
operands.variableUnitLabel: read request units for LOCAL_QUORUM reads

components.label: Number of TTL delete operations
components.placeholder: Enter the number of TTL delete operations
components.description: DELETE operations triggered by the TTL process which deletes expired data. If you do not plan to enable TTL, use zero for number of delete operations

components.label: PITR Storage
components.description: Point-in-time recovery (PITR) helps protect your Amazon Keyspaces tables from accidental write or delete operations by providing you continuous backups of your table data. If you enable it, the amount of PITR storage is exactly the same as your total storage.

templates.title: Keyspaces provisioned capacity
templates.description: With provisioned capacity mode, you specify the number of reads and writes per second that you expect your application to perform. You can increase and decrease the provisioned capacity for your table automatically in response to actual application traffic by enabling auto scaling. Provisioned capacity is a good option for applications with steady traffic. With provisioned capacity you only pay for the reads and write capacity you provision.

components.label: Storage
components.description: Enter the amount of storage for your keyspace in a given month. Note, unlike Cassandra. Amazon Keyspaces only charges for one copy of your data

components.label: Baseline write rate
components.description: Enter number of writes per second that your workload needs during off-peak periods. Write operations include INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations Each write request can write upto 1KB of data

components.label: Peak write rate
components.description: Enter maximum number of writes per second that your workload needs during peak periods. Write operations include INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations. Each write request can write upto 1KB of data

components.label: Duration of peak write activity
components.description: Enter the number of hours per month that your write workload operates at peak

components.outputUnitLabel: total WCU-hours per month for baseline writes
total WCU-hours per month for peak writes

operands.variableUnitLabel: total WCU-hours per month for baseline writes

operands.variableLabel: total WCU-hours per month for peak writes
operands.variableUnitLabel: total WCU-hours per month for peak writes

components.description: LOCAL_ONE consistency optimizes for performance and availability by returning the first returned value from any storage replica. Enter the percentage of your reads that will use LOCAL_ONE consistency

components.label: LOCAL_QUORUM reads
components.description: LOCAL_QUORUM consistency optimizes for data correctness by requiring at least two storage replicas to return a value before it is returned to your application. Enter the percentage of your reads that will use LOCAL_QUORUM consistency

components.label: Duration of peak read activity

components.label: PITR Storage
components.description: Point-in-time recovery (PITR) helps protect your Amazon Keyspaces tables from accidental write or delete operations by providing you continuous backups of your table data. If you enable it, the amount of PITR storage is exactly the same as your total storage.

description.aws: Amazon Keyspaces Change Data Capture (CDC) allows customers to capture a time-ordered sequence of change events on Amazon Keyspaces tables in near-real time with sub-second latency. You are charged based on the GetRecord API requests per month.

components.label: Total number of Keyspaces CDC GetRecord API requests

operands.variableLabel: Total number of Keyspaces CDC GetRecord API requests
